conditions.title: Back Pain
conditions.description: For both sudden-onset and long-term back pain, acupuncture and TCM therapies offer meaningful relief for lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica — all without relying on drugs or surgery.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, back pain is understood as a disruption of Qi and blood flow through the body's channels. Our approach combines acupuncture to clear these blockages, cupping therapy to enhance circulation, and herbal medicine to ease inflammation and support back strength.

conditions.title: Neck & Shoulder Pain
conditions.description: Tension, stiffness, and aching in the neck and shoulders — whether from stress, poor posture, or injury — can all be meaningfully addressed through TCM care.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, neck and shoulder pain commonly reflects Qi stagnation and blood stasis in the affected area. We address this by using acupuncture to release trigger points, Tui Na to ease muscle tightness, and cupping to promote better circulation.

conditions.title: Arthritis & Joint Pain
symptoms: Pain that intensifies with changes in weather
conditions.description: Natural approaches to managing pain associated with osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and general joint inflammation.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM typically classifies arthritis as a 'Bi Syndrome,' where pathogenic factors such as wind, cold, dampness, or heat obstruct the joints. Treatment is directed at clearing these factors, calming inflammation, and building up the body's overall resilience.

conditions.title: Headaches & Migraines
conditions.description: Tension headaches, migraines, and persistent head pain can all be approached through TCM therapies, offering meaningful support without relying on medication.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM recognizes several distinct headache patterns — among them Liver Yang rising, Blood deficiency, and invasion by external pathogenic factors. After identifying your individual pattern, we design a personalized treatment plan using acupuncture and herbal medicine.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorders
conditions.description: Compassionate, medication-free support for anxiety, panic attacks, and persistent feelings of nervousness.
conditions.tcmApproach: Anxiety is frequently linked in TCM to Heart and Kidney disharmony, Liver Qi stagnation, or disturbance of the Shen (spirit). Our treatments work to calm the Shen, restore smooth Qi flow, and nourish the underlying organ systems to bring emotional steadiness back.

conditions.title: Depression & Low Mood
symptoms: A sense of hopelessness or despair
conditions.description: Whole-person support for depression, persistent low energy, and a diminished sense of motivation.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, depression can be linked to Liver Qi stagnation, Heart Blood deficiency, or Spleen Qi deficiency. Care is directed toward freeing stagnant Qi, addressing underlying deficiencies, and uplifting the spirit.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Disorders
conditions.description: Supporting those who struggle to fall asleep, stay asleep, or achieve restorative sleep quality through natural approaches.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, disrupted sleep is commonly traced to Heart and Kidney imbalance, Yin deficiency with internal heat, or disturbance of the Shen. Our approach nourishes Yin, settles the Heart, and anchors the Shen to support deeper, more restful sleep.

conditions.title: Stress & Burnout
conditions.description: When chronic stress or burnout has worn you down, we work with you to rebuild balance and strengthen your resilience.
conditions.tcmApproach: Prolonged stress depletes the body's Qi and leads to Liver stagnation. We address this by regulating Liver function, tonifying deficiencies, and incorporating stress management strategies that help restore equilibrium and support lasting resilience.

conditions.title: IBS & Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Caring support for those living with irritable bowel syndrome, bloating, gas, and digestive discomfort.
conditions.tcmApproach: IBS is often understood in TCM as Liver-Spleen disharmony, where emotional stress disrupts digestive function. Treatment works to regulate Liver Qi, fortify the Spleen, and bring the digestive system back into harmony.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux & GERD
conditions.description: Supportive care for heartburn, acid reflux, and gastroesophageal reflux disease using natural treatment approaches.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, reflux is seen as Stomach Qi rebelling upward, frequently accompanied by heat or phlegm. Our treatment aims to redirect Stomach Qi downward, clear excess heat, and reinforce healthy digestive function.

conditions.title: Constipation
symptoms: Less frequent bowel movements than usual
conditions.description: Thoughtful, effective support for those dealing with chronic constipation or irregular bowel movements.
conditions.tcmApproach: Constipation can arise from several TCM patterns, including Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, Yin deficiency, or Spleen Qi weakness. Your care plan is tailored to your individual pattern, with the goal of restoring healthy, regular bowel function.

conditions.title: Fertility Support
conditions.description: Wide-ranging support for both women and men pursuing natural conception or preparing for IVF.
conditions.tcmApproach: Our approach centers on regulating menstrual cycles, nourishing Kidney Essence (Jing), enhancing blood flow to the reproductive organs, and easing stress. Each partner receives a treatment plan tailored to their unique needs.

conditions.title: Menstrual Disorders
conditions.description: Natural care for painful periods, irregular cycles, PMS, and a range of menstrual-related concerns.
conditions.tcmApproach: Menstrual concerns are frequently connected to Liver Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, or Cold in the uterus. We work to regulate cycles, nourish Blood, move Qi freely, and clear pathogenic factors that disrupt balance.

conditions.title: Menopause Symptoms
conditions.description: Compassionate, natural support for hot flashes, night sweats, mood shifts, and the range of symptoms that can accompany menopause.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM views menopause as a gradual decline of Kidney Yin and Yang. By nourishing Kidney Yin, harmonizing Yin and Yang, and calming the Shen, we aim to ease this life transition and help reduce associated symptoms.

conditions.title: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)
conditions.description: A whole-person approach to supporting hormone balance, restoring regular cycles, and managing PCOS symptoms.
conditions.tcmApproach: PCOS is understood in TCM as involving Kidney deficiency, Phlegm-Damp accumulation, and Liver Qi stagnation. Treatment is focused on resolving phlegm, moving and regulating Qi, and supporting Kidney function.

conditions.title: Allergies & Hay Fever
conditions.description: Natural care for seasonal allergies, hay fever, and sensitivities to environmental triggers.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM associates allergies with weakness in Wei Qi (defensive Qi) alongside internal imbalances. Our treatment strengthens Wei Qi, clears pathogenic factors, and works to reduce the body's inflammatory responses.

conditions.title: Asthma
conditions.description: Complementary care to support asthma management, with a focus on reducing how often and how severely episodes occur.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, asthma is linked to deficiency in the Lung and Kidney systems alongside an accumulation of phlegm. Our approach focuses on supporting Lung and Kidney function, clearing phlegm, and helping to calm the body's reactivity.

conditions.title: Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Care for a persistent cough that has not improved with conventional treatments.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, chronic cough can arise from several distinct patterns — among them Lung Yin deficiency, Lung Heat, or an accumulation of phlegm. By carefully identifying which pattern is present, we tailor treatment to address your specific underlying imbalance.
treatmentMethods: Herbal remedies to soothe the throat

conditions.title: Frequent Colds & Flu
conditions.description: Support your immune system to help reduce how often you get sick and how severe colds and respiratory infections feel when they occur.
conditions.tcmApproach: Getting sick often is a sign, in TCM terms, of Wei Qi deficiency. We work to strengthen Wei Qi, support Lung and Spleen function, and build a more resilient immune foundation over time.

conditions.title: Chronic Fatigue
symptoms: Struggling to get through everyday tasks
conditions.description: When persistent tiredness and low energy are affecting your quality of life, we focus on supporting your body's ability to recover and rebuild vitality.
conditions.tcmApproach: Chronic fatigue often points to Qi deficiency in the Spleen, the Kidneys, or both — and may also involve Blood deficiency. We work to address these underlying deficiencies and help restore your energy and sense of wellbeing.
treatmentMethods: Guidance on balancing rest and physical activity

introduction.note: Not seeing your condition here? Reach out to us — there's a good chance we can still support you.
introduction.text: Here at Dr. Maya Chen Clinic, our care goes beyond managing symptoms — we look at the whole person and work to understand the root causes of illness, while also addressing the discomfort you're experiencing right now. The conditions listed below represent some of the areas we most commonly support.
